sources.text_excerpt: Office of Public Affairs | Federal Grand Jury Charges Southern Poverty Law Center for Wire Fraud, False Statements, and Conspiracy to Commit Money Laundering | United States Department of Justice Skip to main content An official website of the United States government Here's how you know Here's how you know Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in
sources.title: Federal Grand Jury Charges Southern Poverty Law Center for Wire Fraud, False Statements, and Conspiracy to Commit Money Laundering

summary: DOJ alleges SPLC diverted donor funds to extremist-linked individuals through covert accounts and fictitious entities.
DOJ alleges the nonprofit retained grant money by submitting false reports about events, services, and beneficiaries.

agencies.name: Department of Health and Human Services

primary_entity_name: CENTER FOR COMMUNITY ACADEMIC SUCCESS PARTNERSHIPS
summary: Executive sentenced for multiple nonprofit fraud schemes involving inflated grant budgets, sham vendors, and AmeriCorps misuse.

usaspending.recipient_name: CENTER FOR COMMUNITY ACADEMIC SUCCESS PARTNERSHIPS

summary: DOJ says a nonprofit executive diverted beneficiary funds, hid the theft in false ledgers, and laundered proceeds through personal purchases.

primary_entity_name: CENTER FOR SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST ADMINISTRATION
summary: DOJ alleges a nonprofit handling special-needs trusts was used as a slush fund and concealed losses with false account statements.

usaspending.recipient_name: THE CENTER FOR SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST ADMINISTRATION, INC.

summary: DOJ alleges county grant funds were steered and concealed through controlled entities and false certifications.
Funds earmarked for nonprofit missions were diverted and covered with fake minutes, fake invoices, and a fraudulent grant application.

primary_entity_name: WASHINGTON COALITION OF CRIME VICTIM ADVOCATES
summary: DOJ says the executive extracted salary and fabricated invoices for trainings that never occurred.

primary_entity_name: BLACK LIVES MATTER OF GREATER ATLANTA
summary: Sentencing case involving donation diversion, property-related concealment, and money laundering.
DOJ alleges donations solicited for a memorial charity purpose were used for personal and family expenses.
CEO charged with embezzling federal grant funds for personal spending including wedding costs, travel, and crypto.

agencies.name: Department of Health and Human Services

summary: Former nonprofit leader pleaded guilty after diverting donations and causing false Form 990 filings.
DOJ charged a charity operator for diverting millions using nominee structures, related management companies, and multiple charities.
Founders admitted using a nonprofit to defraud donors and evade taxes.
Grant-funded victim-services nonprofit case involving unauthorized payments and travel-related fraud despite prior warning signs.
Finance director admitted diverting federal and private grant funds through forged checks and an insider-controlled entity.

agencies.name: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development
Corporation for National and Community Service

summary: Former nonprofit health-clinic CEO convicted of routing federal grant funds through companies he controlled.

agencies.name: Department of Health and Human Services

summary: Charity director admitted using donor and grant funds for extensive personal expenses and filing false tax returns.
DOJ alleged a charity executive diverted funds through a second nonprofit and false scholarship-related representations.

primary_entity_name: KEELY'S DISTRICT BOXING AND YOUTH CENTER
summary: Executive director admitted using youth-program grants for gambling and other personal expenses.
Sentencing in a long-running charity fraud scheme involving phony representations and personal luxury spending.
Founder charged with stealing donations and using charity funds for travel, entertainment, and personal expenses.
